.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:1431:    27	**Critical Dependencies**: consolidated research findings `CF-004`, `CF-010`, `CF-014`, `CF-021`, `CF-027`, and `CF-030`; existing deep-loop reducer and parity tests; packet-local, append-only state discipline.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:1450:    46	| **Successor** | `../002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md` (Phase 002 — semantic coverage graph); downstream Phase 003 (wave executor), Phase 004 (offline loop optimizer) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:1569:   165	| REQ-002a | Deep research and deep review MUST only treat STOP as legal when convergence math, coverage expectations, and binary quality gates all pass together. | The legal-stop design explicitly requires convergence, coverage, and quality gates to pass as one combined decision; STOP is never treated as legal based on novelty math alone, and the `legalStop.gateResults` record shows the full gate bundle. | `CF-004` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:102-111`], `CF-010` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:168-177`] |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:1570:   166	| REQ-002b | When a loop wants to STOP but cannot legally stop, it MUST persist a first-class blocked-stop event with reason, gate results, and recovery path, and that event MUST be replayable. | If convergence math votes STOP but coverage or quality gates fail, the workflow records a first-class `blockedStop` event with `stopReason`, `legalStop.gateResults`, and recovery metadata, leaves `status` non-terminal, and can replay the blocked-stop decision from packet-local artifacts without treating it as a mere continue side effect. | `CF-004` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:102-111`], `CF-010` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:168-177`] |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:1574:   170	| REQ-006 | The implementation plan MUST shift loop confidence from structure-only checks to behavior-first tests that exercise narrow questions, evidence citations, conclusion confidence, convergence, and stop-reason persistence. | The planned test stack includes dedicated behavioral suites for deep research and deep review plus extensions to reducer/parity tests; passing behavior tests becomes part of the done gate for these runtime changes. | `CF-004` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:102-111`], `Theme T-005` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:404-406`] |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-strategy.md:15:Closing quality audit of the entire 042 packet after phase 008 shipped. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2838:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/spec.md:121:| REQ-005 | Phase 4b must remain explicitly deferred until replay fixtures, behavioral suites, and corpus prerequisites exist. | [./004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md](./004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2840:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:20:## ADR-001: Ship deterministic offline config optimization first and keep promotion advisory-only
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2841:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:35:We needed a safe way to improve deep-loop runtime configs using real traces, but the repo did not yet have the replay fixtures, behavioral suites, or multi-family corpus coverage needed for broad prompt or meta-optimization. The phase had to produce real value now without pretending the wider optimizer vision was already safe, and it had to stop the optimizer from mutating locked contract fields or applying production config changes directly.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2842:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:49:**We chose**: deliver Phase 4a as a deterministic offline config optimizer now, keep Phase 4b prompt/meta optimization explicitly deferred, and bound all current promotion output to advisory-only candidate patches or reports.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2843:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:61:| **Deterministic config optimization now with advisory-only promotion** | Delivers real value with a clear safety envelope | Leaves some ambitious optimization ideas intentionally deferred and still requires human review | 9/10 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2844:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:62:| Build prompt and meta optimization immediately | Bigger theoretical upside | Unsafe without replay fixtures, behavioral suites, and multi-family data | 3/10 |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2845:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:88:| Advisory candidates are treated as production-ready anyway | H | Make advisory-only status explicit across spec, plan, tasks, checklist, and implementation summary |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2846:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:3:description: "Define an offline replay optimizer that tunes deterministic deep-loop configs against real packet traces now, while deferring prompt and meta-optimization until replay fixtures, behavioral suites, and broader corpus coverage exist."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2847:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:22:Phase 004 is now explicitly split into two sub-phases. Phase 4a is the realistic near-term deliverable: build an offline compile/evaluate loop that replays real packet traces, tunes deterministic numeric config thresholds, and emits advisory patch recommendations with a full audit trail. Phase 4b is deferred future work for prompt-pack and meta-optimization once replay fixtures, behavioral suites, and multi-family corpus coverage actually exist.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2848:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:39:| **Handoff Criteria** | Phase 4a outputs are replay-verified, audit-trailed, and emitted as advisory-only candidate patches until Phase 1 replay fixtures and Phase 3 behavioral suites exist. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2849:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:48:- Phase 001 replay fixtures must exist before any production-grade replay or promotion claim is credible.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2850:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:49:- Phase 003 behavioral suites must exist before any non-advisory promotion is allowed.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2851:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:69:Define an offline replay optimizer that learns from real packet traces, scores run quality against a shared rubric, searches bounded deterministic config space, and emits advisory candidate patches only. Production promotion remains out of scope until Phase 1 replay fixtures and Phase 3 behavioral suites exist, and prompt or meta-optimization remains deferred future work.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2852:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:84:- Add an advisory-only promotion gate that never mutates production config directly while prerequisite fixtures and suites are still missing.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2853:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:85:- Record a full audit trail for every optimization attempt, including rejected candidates and patch-style recommendations.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2854:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:100:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-corpus.cjs` | Create | Build deterministic replay datasets from historical packet artifacts. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2855:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:103:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-runner.cjs` | Create | Replay reducer and convergence logic against alternate configurations. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2856:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:104:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/promote.cjs` | Create | Compare candidate results to baseline and emit advisory-only candidate patches. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2857:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:105:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/optimizer-manifest.json` | Create | Canonical manifest of tunable fields, locked contract fields, and future prompt-pack entrypoints. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2858:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:112:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/optimizer-replay-corpus.vitest.ts` | Create | Verify corpus extraction and fixture normalization. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2859:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:115:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/optimizer-replay-runner.vitest.ts` | Create | Verify deterministic replay under alternate configs. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2860:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:116:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/optimizer-promote.vitest.ts` | Create | Verify advisory promotion gating, rollback behavior, and audit output. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2861:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:117:| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/fixtures/deep-loop-optimizer/` | Create | Packet-family replay fixtures, optimization-result snapshots, and advisory patch artifacts. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2862:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:134:| REQ-006 | No production promotion is allowed until the replay fixtures planned in Phase 1 and the behavioral suites planned in Phase 3 actually exist. | Until both prerequisite surfaces exist, all optimizer outputs are advisory-only candidate patches or reports and must not directly mutate canonical production configs. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2863:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:141:| REQ-008 | Every optimization run MUST emit an audit trail that records what was tried, what improved, what regressed, and why a candidate was accepted or rejected. | Audit output exists for every run, including failed searches and rejected advisory promotions, and captures both candidate settings and evaluation outcomes. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2864:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:156:- **SC-005**: Promotion remains advisory-only until replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2865:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:157:- **SC-006**: Every optimization run produces a reviewable audit trail.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2866:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:168:6. **Given** a candidate outperforms baseline in replay but the Phase 1 replay fixtures or Phase 3 behavioral suites do not yet exist, **when** promotion runs, **then** the optimizer emits only an advisory patch or report and refuses production promotion.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2867:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:169:7. **Given** an older packet trace lacks graph or wave metrics, **when** scoring runs, **then** the audit trail marks those dimensions unavailable rather than inventing placeholder values.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2868:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:182:| Dependency | Phase 001 replay fixtures must exist before promotion can graduate beyond advisory reports | High | Treat missing replay fixtures as a hard promotion block and keep outputs patch-only. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2869:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:183:| Dependency | Phase 003 behavioral suites remain the promotion gate | High | Treat those suites as mandatory pass conditions, not advisory checks. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2870:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:185:| Risk | Optimization without behavioral tests could promote broken configs | High | Keep promotion advisory-only until the behavioral suite exists and passes. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2871:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:205:- Advisory-only promotion must be the default behavior until the prerequisite replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2872:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:222:- A candidate improves replay score before behavioral suites exist. The optimizer must emit only an advisory report or patch artifact and refuse production promotion.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2873:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:234:| **Governance** | High: advisory-only promotion, manifest boundaries, and rollback semantics must stay explicit. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2874:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:235:| **Deferred Scope** | Very High: prompt optimization and meta-learning are materially premature until behavioral suites and broader corpus coverage exist. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2875:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:247:| Missing behavioral tests would allow broken configs through | Medium | High | Keep promotion advisory-only until those suites exist and pass. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2876:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:255:- As a release reviewer, I want every candidate to show baseline comparison, audit reasoning, and explicit advisory-only status until replay and behavioral gates are real.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2878:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/plan.md:3:description: "Deliver Phase 4a as a deterministic offline config optimizer with advisory-only outputs now, and defer Phase 4b prompt/meta optimization until replay fixtures, behavioral suites, and broader corpus coverage exist."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2879:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/plan.md:29:| **Storage** | Replay corpus fixtures, audit records, candidate config snapshots, existing deep-loop config files |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2880:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/plan.md:34:This phase now splits into two tracks. Phase 4a is the realistic near-term work: harvest real traces, score them with a rubric, search bounded deterministic config space, replay candidates deterministically, and emit advisory candidate patches with a full audit trail. Phase 4b remains deliberately deferred until replay fixtures, behavioral suites, and multi-family corpus coverage exist for safe prompt and meta-optimization.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2881:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/plan.md:48:- [x] Phase 001 replay fixtures exist for production-grade replay gating.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2936:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/implementation-summary.md:35:Deep-loop configs can now be tuned offline against real packet traces without experimenting live in production. Phase 4a delivers a complete compile/evaluate loop: harvest traces, score them with a rubric, search bounded deterministic config space, replay candidates, and emit advisory candidate patches with a full audit trail.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2937:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/implementation-summary.md:59:The promotion gate refuses production mutation until replay fixtures and behavioral suites exist. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2974:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6552:    53	The current deep-loop stack is functional, but it still treats "converged" as a mostly inferred state instead of a strongly explained runtime truth. Deep research and deep review already have JSONL state, dashboards, and reducer/parity coverage, yet the consolidated research shows clear gaps around stop-reason taxonomy, legal done gates, resume/start-from behavior, research claim verification, audit trails, richer dashboards, behavior-first test coverage, and large-run coordination.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2984:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:7486:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:22:Phase 004 builds the offline compile/evaluate loop for deep research and deep review. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:2985:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:7490:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:55:- Deterministic replay runner and promotion gate with full audit trail.

I’m now tightening the tunable-surface inventory by pulling every threshold, switch, and prompt-bearing file that Phase 4 would realistically optimize, including the agent markdown surfaces the spec hints at.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:5799:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6192:I’ve now confirmed a key feasibility tension: Phase 4 assumes a multi-family replay corpus, but the actual loop telemetry currently appears concentrated in `040`, while `028` and `042` mostly contribute specs/docs rather than run ledgers. I’m quantifying that gap and summarizing the 040 dataset shape so we can judge whether optimization is realistic now or needs more corpus-building first.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:5800:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6648:   149	| `.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/fixtures/deep-loop-replay/` | Create | Seed replay corpus from packet families `028`, `040`, and `042` for invalid-state, resume, completed-continue, and compaction-equivalence fixtures. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:5801:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6815:    95	  -> add replay corpus, thin end-to-end harnesses, reducer/schema extensions, and integration fixtures early
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:5802:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:7089:One important implementation reality just surfaced: `reduce-state.cjs` is a synchronizer, not a convergence evaluator. It reads the stored JSONL and iteration files, then publishes registry/dashboard state, but it does not recompute stop logic from thresholds itself, which means an offline optimizer would need a new replay evaluator rather than tuning the reducer in isolation.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:5819:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:8864:- External evidence supports a staged strategy: [Random Search for Hyper-Parameter Optimization](https://www.jmlr.org/papers/v13/bergstra12a.html) favors random search over grid in large spaces, while [Practical Bayesian Optimization of Machine Learning Algorithms](https://lips.cs.princeton.edu/bibliography/snoek2012practical/) becomes attractive once evaluations are expensive and the objective is stable.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:5820:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:8865:Novel insight: the right sequence is not grid vs Bayesian; it is tiny deterministic sweeps first, then bounded random search, then BO only after the replay objective is trustworthy.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6378:Finding ID: `F-006-001`  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6385:Evidence: `savePromotionReport()` derives `dir = path.dirname(outputPath)`, creates it recursively, then calls `fs.writeFileSync(outputPath, ...)` with no root, symlink, or file-class checks. This contradicts [004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:134) and [decision-record.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/decision-record.md:35), which require no direct production mutation.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6388:Finding ID: `F-006-002`  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6395:Evidence: `sampleConfig()` blindly iterates `Object.entries(paramSpace)`, and `randomSearch()` passes that output straight into evaluation and the audit trail. Direct probe result: `{"stopReason":1,"convergenceThreshold":0.99}` was accepted from a custom parameter space, even though the manifest marks `stopReason` locked and caps `convergenceThreshold` at `0.20` in [optimizer-manifest.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/optimizer-manifest.json:59). That violates [REQ-007](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:140) and the ADR claim that search mutates only manifest-managed numeric fields.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6398:Finding ID: `F-006-003`  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6405:Evidence: The code only runs `checkManifestBoundary()` inside `if (opts.manifest)`. Direct probe result: passing `{ config: { stopReason: 'converged' }, score: { composite: 0.9 } }` without a manifest returned `decision:"advisory-accept"` and `manifestCheck:null`. That breaks acceptance scenario 8 in [004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:170) and the ADR requirement for an explicit manifest boundary.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-006.md:6408:Finding ID: `F-006-004`

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:582:   165	| REQ-002a | Deep research and deep review MUST only treat STOP as legal when convergence math, coverage expectations, and binary quality gates all pass together. | The legal-stop design explicitly requires convergence, coverage, and quality gates to pass as one combined decision; STOP is never treated as legal based on novelty math alone, and the `legalStop.gateResults` record shows the full gate bundle. | `CF-004` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:102-111`], `CF-010` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:168-177`] |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:583:   166	| REQ-002b | When a loop wants to STOP but cannot legally stop, it MUST persist a first-class blocked-stop event with reason, gate results, and recovery path, and that event MUST be replayable. | If convergence math votes STOP but coverage or quality gates fail, the workflow records a first-class `blockedStop` event with `stopReason`, `legalStop.gateResults`, and recovery metadata, leaves `status` non-terminal, and can replay the blocked-stop decision from packet-local artifacts without treating it as a mere continue side effect. | `CF-004` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:102-111`], `CF-010` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:168-177`] |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:587:   170	| REQ-006 | The implementation plan MUST shift loop confidence from structure-only checks to behavior-first tests that exercise narrow questions, evidence citations, conclusion confidence, convergence, and stop-reason persistence. | The planned test stack includes dedicated behavioral suites for deep research and deep review plus extensions to reducer/parity tests; passing behavior tests becomes part of the done gate for these runtime changes. | `CF-004` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:102-111`], `Theme T-005` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:404-406`] |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:3045:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:194:**Optional fields:** `parentSessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `findingsRefined`, `findingRefs`, `traceabilityChecks`, `coverage`, `noveltyJustification`, `ruledOut`, `focusTrack`, `scoreEstimate`, `segment`, `convergenceSignals`, `graphEvents`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:3075:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/convergence.md:130:Triggers when all 4 dimensions (correctness, security, traceability, maintainability) are covered, no active P0/P1 remains, at least 1 stabilization pass has occurred, and the 5-gate legal-stop bundle passes. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1423:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/plan.md:109:| **Phase 3: Wave Executor** | Prove fan-out/join, add deterministic and graph-enhanced segmentation, activate large-target wave mode, preserve keyed merge and board ownership | 8-10 focused sessions | Phases 1-2 | Fan-out/join proof, segment planner, `board.json`, keyed merge, wave resume tests | [./003-wave-executor/plan.md](./003-wave-executor/plan.md) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1424:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/plan.md:110:| **Phase 4: Offline Loop Optimizer** | `4a` deterministic replay/config tuning now; `4b` prompt/meta optimizer deferred | 3-4 focused sessions for `4a`; `4b` TBD | Phases 1-3 | Replay corpus, rubric, deterministic replay runner, advisory-only promotion, deferred-track guardrails | [./004-offline-loop-optimizer/plan.md](./004-offline-loop-optimizer/plan.md) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1452:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/spec.md:46:| **Successor** | `../002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md` (Phase 002 — semantic coverage graph); downstream Phase 003 (wave executor), Phase 004 (offline loop optimizer) |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1634:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:22:Phase 003 adds orchestrator-managed parallel wave execution so deep research and deep review can handle very large targets without abandoning the LEAF-worker model. It formalizes deterministic segmentation, packet-local coordination, segment-level convergence, and auditable merge behavior on top of the coverage-graph foundation from Phase 002.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1635:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:39:| **Predecessor** | `../002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md` |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1636:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:44:**Phase Context**: This phase adds orchestrator-managed parallel batches on top of the coverage-graph substrate from Phase 002. The goal is scale without architectural drift: the workers stay LEAF, while orchestration logic handles segmentation, fan-out, pruning, promotion, and merge.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1637:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:91:- Define segment-local JSONL or state artifacts and deterministic merge rules into the main packet lineage using explicit keys: `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1638:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:144:| REQ-001 | The phase MUST define two segmentation versions for both large review scopes and large research scopes. | v1 uses deterministic heuristics such as file-count thresholds, directory grouping, simple hotspot ranking, domain counts, authority levels, and stable cluster assignment; v2 explicitly layers Phase 002 coverage-graph or cluster signals on top of v1 once the graph surfaces are operational. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1639:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:147:| REQ-004 | Segment-local JSONL and merge rules MUST preserve auditability when wave results are merged back into the main packet lineage. | Segment artifacts remain replayable on their own, merged records always include explicit keys `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, and merge logic never relies on append order as the source of truth. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1640:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:153:| REQ-005 | Each segment MUST use Phase 002 graph signals independently for convergence, pruning, promotion, and v2 decomposition upgrades. | Segment convergence explicitly references coverage-graph status or convergence outputs; prune logic can retire converged or stuck segments without dropping unresolved global gaps silently; graph-enhanced segmentation is blocked until the Phase 002 coverage graph is operational. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:1641:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:284:- Predecessor phase: `../002-semantic-coverage-graph/spec.md`

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2129:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2268:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6663:   164	| REQ-004 | Both loops MUST produce append-only audit journals that capture file reads, searches, evidence extraction, focus/dimension rotation decisions, and convergence-calculation inputs. | Runtime artifacts include a canonical journal file per session, it is append-only, reducer/dashboard flows can summarize it, and stop/recovery analysis can be reproduced from packet artifacts without hidden state. | `CF-010` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:168-177`] |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2312:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2847:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/implementation-summary.md:35:Deep research and deep review can now handle very large targets through orchestrator-managed parallel wave execution without abandoning the LEAF-worker model. The implementation adds deterministic segmentation, packet-local coordination, segment-level convergence, and auditable merge behavior on top of the Phase 002 coverage-graph foundation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2313:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2851:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/implementation-summary.md:55:The JSONL merge contract uses explicit key ordering by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId` rather than append order. Segment provenance, dedupe, and conflict metadata survive repeated merges. Segment JSONL lineage is deterministic and auditable.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2314:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2909:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:22:Phase 003 adds orchestrator-managed parallel wave execution so deep research and deep review can handle very large targets without abandoning the LEAF-worker model. It formalizes deterministic segmentation, packet-local coordination, segment-level convergence, and auditable merge behavior on top of the coverage-graph foundation from Phase 002.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2315:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2913:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:45:**Phase Context**: This phase adds orchestrator-managed parallel batches on top of the coverage-graph substrate from Phase 002. The goal is scale without architectural drift: the workers stay LEAF, while orchestration logic handles segmentation, fan-out, pruning, promotion, and merge.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2316:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2927:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:91:- Define segment-local JSONL or state artifacts and deterministic merge rules into the main packet lineage using explicit keys: `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2317:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2957:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:147:| REQ-004 | Segment-local JSONL and merge rules MUST preserve auditability when wave results are merged back into the main packet lineage. | Segment artifacts remain replayable on their own, merged records always include explicit keys `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId`, and merge logic never relies on append order as the source of truth. |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:2318:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2958:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:153:| REQ-005 | Each segment MUST use Phase 002 graph signals independently for convergence, pruning, promotion, and v2 decomposition upgrades. | Segment convergence explicitly references coverage-graph status or convergence outputs; prune logic can retire converged or stuck segments without dropping unresolved global gaps silently; graph-enhanced segmentation is blocked until the Phase 002 coverage graph is operational. |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3779:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/implementation-summary.md:35:Deep research and deep review can now handle very large targets through orchestrator-managed parallel wave execution without abandoning the LEAF-worker model. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-002.md:3780:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/implementation-summary.md:55:The JSONL merge contract uses explicit key ordering by `sessionId`, `generation`, `segment`, `wave`, and `findingId` rather than append order. Segment provenance, dedupe, and conflict metadata survive repeated merges. Segment JSONL lineage is deterministic and auditable.

     1	'use strict';
     2	
     3	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     4	// MODULE: Wave Lifecycle (T-WE-NEW-1, T004)
     5	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     6	// Orchestrator lifecycle helpers for fan-out, join, prune, promote,
     7	// merge, and resume state transitions. Parallelism lives at the
     8	// orchestrator layer; LEAF agents remain non-spawning workers.
     9	//
    10	// Fan-out/join is implemented as helper-module orchestration that
    11	// safely performs parallel dispatch outside the YAML surface. The
    12	// YAML workflow engine delegates to these helpers rather than
    13	// requiring native parallel-step support.
    14	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
    15	
    16	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
    17	   1. CONSTANTS
    18	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    19	
    20	/**
    21	 * Valid wave lifecycle phases in execution order.
    22	 * @type {ReadonlyArray<string>}
    23	 */
    24	const LIFECYCLE_PHASES = Object.freeze([
    25	  'prepass',
    26	  'plan',
    27	  'fan-out',
    28	  'prune',
    29	  'promote',
    30	  'join',
    31	  'merge',
    32	]);
    33	
    34	/**
    35	 * Valid segment statuses.
    36	 * @type {ReadonlyArray<string>}
    37	 */
    38	const SEGMENT_STATUSES = Object.freeze([
    39	  'pending',
    40	  'dispatched',
    41	  'running',
    42	  'converged',
    43	  'pruned',
    44	  'failed',
    45	  'completed',
    46	]);
    47	
    48	/**
    49	 * Maximum number of parallel segments per wave.
    50	 * @type {number}
    51	 */
    52	const MAX_PARALLEL_SEGMENTS = 8;
    53	
    54	/**
    55	 * Check whether a value is a non-empty string.
    56	 * @param {unknown} value
    57	 * @returns {boolean}
    58	 */
    59	function isNonEmptyString(value) {
    60	  return typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length > 0;
    61	}
    62	
    63	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
    64	   2. FAN-OUT/JOIN PROOF
    65	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    66	
    67	/**
    68	 * Check if the current workflow supports parallel dispatch.
    69	 * Returns a proof object describing the orchestration path.
    70	 *
    71	 * This is the canonical proof that wave execution has a viable
    72	 * parallel dispatch mechanism before any wave-mode runtime build.
    73	 *
    74	 * @param {object} workflowEngine - Description of the workflow engine capabilities
    75	 * @param {boolean} [workflowEngine.hasNativeParallel] - Whether engine supports parallel steps natively
    76	 * @param {boolean} [workflowEngine.hasHelperOrchestration] - Whether helper-module orchestration is available
    77	 * @returns {{ canFanOut: boolean, method: string, proof: string, maxParallel: number }}
    78	 */
    79	function canFanOut(workflowEngine) {
    80	  if (!workflowEngine || typeof workflowEngine !== 'object') {
    81	    return {
    82	      canFanOut: false,
    83	      method: 'none',
    84	      proof: 'No workflow engine provided',
    85	      maxParallel: 0,
    86	    };
    87	  }
    88	
    89	  // Prefer helper-module orchestration (proven path for current YAML engine)
    90	  if (workflowEngine.hasHelperOrchestration) {
    91	    return {
    92	      canFanOut: true,
    93	      method: 'helper-module',
    94	      proof: 'Helper-module orchestration wraps YAML engine for safe fan-out/join',
    95	      maxParallel: MAX_PARALLEL_SEGMENTS,
    96	    };
    97	  }
    98	
    99	  // Native parallel dispatch (future engine extension)
   100	  if (workflowEngine.hasNativeParallel) {
   101	    return {
   102	      canFanOut: true,
   103	      method: 'native',
   104	      proof: 'YAML engine supports native parallel step dispatch',
   105	      maxParallel: MAX_PARALLEL_SEGMENTS,
   106	    };
   107	  }
   108	
   109	  return {
   110	    canFanOut: false,
   111	    method: 'none',
   112	    proof: 'Neither helper orchestration nor native parallel dispatch available',
   113	    maxParallel: 0,
   114	  };
   115	}
   116	
   117	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   118	   3. WAVE CONTEXT
   119	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   120	
   121	/**
   122	 * Create a wave execution context for tracking lifecycle state.
   123	 *
   124	 * @param {string} target - The review/research target identifier
   125	 * @param {'review'|'research'} loopType - Type of deep loop
   126	 * @param {object} [options] - Additional configuration
   127	 * @param {string} [options.sessionId] - Session identifier (auto-generated if absent)
   128	 * @param {number} [options.generation] - Generation counter (default: 1)
   129	 * @returns {object} Wave context object
   130	 */
   131	function createWaveContext(target, loopType, options) {
   132	  if (!isNonEmptyString(target)) return null;
   133	  if (loopType !== 'review' && loopType !== 'research') {
   134	    return null;
   135	  }
   136	
   137	  const opts = options || {};
   138	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   139	
   140	  return {
   141	    target,
   142	    loopType,
   143	    sessionId: opts.sessionId || `wave-${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8)}`,
   144	    generation: typeof opts.generation === 'number' ? opts.generation : 1,
   145	    phase: 'prepass',
   146	    createdAt: now,
   147	    updatedAt: now,
   148	    segments: [],
   149	    waves: [],
   150	    currentWave: null,
   151	    mergedResults: [],
   152	    status: 'initialized',
   153	  };
   154	}
   155	
   156	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   157	   4. FAN-OUT
   158	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   159	
   160	/**
   161	 * Dispatch a wave of segments for parallel execution.
   162	 * Each segment becomes an independent worker dispatch.
   163	 *
   164	 * @param {Array<object>} segments - Segment descriptors from the planner
   165	 * @param {object} config - Wave dispatch configuration
   166	 * @param {string} config.sessionId - Session identifier
   167	 * @param {number} config.generation - Generation counter
   168	 * @param {number} config.waveNumber - Current wave number (1-based)
   169	 * @param {number} [config.maxParallel] - Maximum parallel dispatches
   170	 * @returns {{ waveId: string, dispatches: Array<object>, timestamp: string }}
   171	 */
   172	function dispatchWave(segments, config) {
   173	  if (!Array.isArray(segments) || segments.length === 0) {
   174	    return null;
   175	  }
   176	  if (!config || !isNonEmptyString(config.sessionId) || !config.waveNumber) {
   177	    return null;
   178	  }
   179	
   180	  const maxParallel = config.maxParallel || MAX_PARALLEL_SEGMENTS;
   181	  const now = new Date().toISOString();
   182	  const waveId = `${config.sessionId}-w${config.waveNumber}`;
   183	
   184	  // Cap segments to maxParallel
   185	  const activeSegments = segments.slice(0, maxParallel);
   186	
   187	  const dispatches = activeSegments.map((segment, index) => ({
   188	    dispatchId: `${waveId}-s${index}`,
   189	    waveId,
   190	    segmentId: segment.segmentId || `seg-${index}`,
   191	    segmentIndex: index,
   192	    status: 'dispatched',
   193	    dispatchedAt: now,
   194	    completedAt: null,
   195	    result: null,
   196	    error: null,
   197	  }));
   198	
   199	  return {
   200	    waveId,
   201	    dispatches,
   202	    timestamp: now,
   203	    sessionId: config.sessionId,
   204	    generation: config.generation || 1,
   205	    waveNumber: config.waveNumber,
   206	    totalSegments: segments.length,
   207	    activeSegments: activeSegments.length,
   208	    deferredSegments: Math.max(0, segments.length - maxParallel),
   209	  };
   210	}
   211	
   212	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   213	   5. JOIN
   214	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   215	
   216	/**
   217	 * Merge strategy definitions for joining wave results.
   218	 * @type {Readonly<Record<string, string>>}
   219	 */
   220	const MERGE_STRATEGIES = Object.freeze({
   221	  CONCAT: 'concat',
   222	  DEDUPE: 'dedupe',
   223	  PRIORITY: 'priority',
   224	});
   225	
   226	/**
   227	 * Join results from parallel workers back into a single result set.
   228	 * Applies the specified merge strategy to handle duplicates and conflicts.
   229	 *
   230	 * @param {Array<object>} results - Results from completed segment dispatches
   231	 * @param {string} [mergeStrategy='dedupe'] - How to merge: 'concat', 'dedupe', or 'priority'
   232	 * @returns {{ merged: Array<object>, conflicts: Array<object>, stats: object }}
   233	 */
   234	function joinWave(results, mergeStrategy) {
   235	  if (!Array.isArray(results)) {
   236	    return { merged: [], conflicts: [], stats: { totalResults: 0, mergedFindings: 0, conflicts: 0 } };
   237	  }
   238	
   239	  const strategy = mergeStrategy || MERGE_STRATEGIES.DEDUPE;
   240	  const conflicts = [];
   241	  const findingMap = new Map();
   242	
   243	  for (const result of results) {
   244	    if (!result || !result.findings) continue;
   245	
   246	    for (const finding of result.findings) {
   247	      const key = buildFindingKey(finding);
   248	
   249	      if (findingMap.has(key)) {
   250	        const existing = findingMap.get(key);
   251	
   252	        if (strategy === MERGE_STRATEGIES.CONCAT) {
   253	          // Keep both under different composite keys
   254	          const altKey = `${key}::${result.segmentId || 'unknown'}`;
   255	          findingMap.set(altKey, {
   256	            ...finding,
   257	            mergedFrom: result.segmentId || 'unknown',
   258	            mergeState: 'appended',
   259	          });
   260	        } else if (strategy === MERGE_STRATEGIES.PRIORITY) {
   261	          // Keep higher severity
   262	          if (compareSeverity(finding.severity, existing.severity) > 0) {
   263	            conflicts.push({
   264	              findingId: key,
   265	              kept: finding,
   266	              replaced: existing,
   267	              reason: 'priority-upgrade',
   268	            });
   269	            findingMap.set(key, {
   270	              ...finding,
   271	              mergedFrom: result.segmentId || 'unknown',
   272	              mergeState: 'promoted',
   273	              priorSegment: existing.mergedFrom,
   274	            });
   275	          } else {
   276	            conflicts.push({
   277	              findingId: key,
   278	              kept: existing,
   279	              replaced: finding,
   280	              reason: 'priority-kept',
   281	            });
   282	          }
   283	        } else {
   284	          // DEDUPE: keep first occurrence, record conflict
   285	          conflicts.push({
   286	            findingId: key,
   287	            kept: existing,
   288	            duplicate: finding,
   289	            duplicateSegment: result.segmentId || 'unknown',
   290	            reason: 'dedupe',
   291	          });
   292	        }
   293	      } else {
   294	        findingMap.set(key, {
   295	          ...finding,
   296	          mergedFrom: result.segmentId || 'unknown',
   297	          mergeState: 'original',
   298	        });
   299	      }
   300	    }
   301	  }
   302	
   303	  const merged = Array.from(findingMap.values());
   304	
   305	  return {
   306	    merged,
   307	    conflicts,
   308	    stats: {
   309	      totalInput: results.reduce((sum, r) => sum + ((r && r.findings) ? r.findings.length : 0), 0),
   310	      totalMerged: merged.length,
   311	      totalConflicts: conflicts.length,
   312	      mergeStrategy: strategy,
   313	    },
   314	  };
   315	}
   316	
   317	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   318	   6. LIFECYCLE TRANSITIONS
   319	----------------------------------------------------------------*/

     2	
     3	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     4	// MODULE: Wave Segment Planner (T001, T-WE-NEW-2, T-WE-NEW-3)
     5	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
     6	// Deterministic segmentation for review files and research domains.
     7	// v1: heuristic-based (file counts, directory grouping, hotspot ranking)
     8	// v2: graph/cluster-enhanced (Phase 002 coverage graph overlay)
     9	//
    10	// Segment identity and ordering are reproducible from the same inputs.
    11	// Activation gates ensure wave mode only activates for large targets.
    12	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
    13	
    14	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
    15	   1. CONSTANTS
    16	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    17	
    18	/**
    19	 * Minimum file count to activate wave mode for review.
    20	 * @type {number}
    21	 */
    22	const REVIEW_FILE_THRESHOLD = 1000;
    23	
    24	/**
    25	 * Minimum domain count to activate wave mode for research.
    26	 * @type {number}
    27	 */
    28	const RESEARCH_DOMAIN_THRESHOLD = 50;
    29	
    30	/**
    31	 * Default maximum files per review segment.
    32	 * @type {number}
    33	 */
    34	const DEFAULT_SEGMENT_SIZE_REVIEW = 200;
    35	
    36	/**
    37	 * Default maximum domains per research segment.
    38	 * @type {number}
    39	 */
    40	const DEFAULT_SEGMENT_SIZE_RESEARCH = 10;
    41	
    42	/**
    43	 * Minimum hotspot spread ratio to justify wave segmentation.
    44	 * At least this fraction of directories must contain hotspots.
    45	 * @type {number}
    46	 */
    47	const HOTSPOT_SPREAD_THRESHOLD = 0.15;
    48	
    49	/**
    50	 * Minimum cluster diversity ratio to justify wave segmentation for research.
    51	 * At least this fraction of domains must span distinct clusters.
    52	 * @type {number}
    53	 */
    54	const CLUSTER_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD = 0.20;
    55	
    56	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
    57	   2. ACTIVATION GATES
    58	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
    59	
    60	/**
    61	 * Check whether wave mode should activate for a review scope.
    62	 * Requires >= REVIEW_FILE_THRESHOLD files with sufficient hotspot spread.
    63	 *
    64	 * @param {Array<object>} files - File inventory list
    65	 * @param {object} [metrics] - Optional precomputed metrics
    66	 * @param {number} [metrics.hotspotSpread] - Fraction of directories with hotspots
    67	 * @returns {{ activate: boolean, reason: string, fileCount: number, hotspotSpread: number }}
    68	 */
    69	function shouldActivateReviewWave(files, metrics) {
    70	  const fileCount = Array.isArray(files) ? files.length : 0;
    71	  const hotspotSpread = (metrics && typeof metrics.hotspotSpread === 'number')
    72	    ? metrics.hotspotSpread
    73	    : computeHotspotSpread(files);
    74	
    75	  if (fileCount < REVIEW_FILE_THRESHOLD) {
    76	    return {
    77	      activate: false,
    78	      reason: `File count ${fileCount} below threshold ${REVIEW_FILE_THRESHOLD}`,
    79	      fileCount,
    80	      hotspotSpread,
    81	    };
    82	  }
    83	
    84	  if (hotspotSpread < HOTSPOT_SPREAD_THRESHOLD) {
    85	    return {
    86	      activate: false,
    87	      reason: `Hotspot spread ${hotspotSpread.toFixed(3)} below threshold ${HOTSPOT_SPREAD_THRESHOLD}`,
    88	      fileCount,
    89	      hotspotSpread,
    90	    };
    91	  }
    92	
    93	  return {
    94	    activate: true,
    95	    reason: `Wave activated: ${fileCount} files, hotspot spread ${hotspotSpread.toFixed(3)}`,
    96	    fileCount,
    97	    hotspotSpread,
    98	  };
    99	}
   100	
   101	/**
   102	 * Check whether wave mode should activate for a research scope.
   103	 * Requires >= RESEARCH_DOMAIN_THRESHOLD domains with sufficient cluster diversity.
   104	 *
   105	 * @param {Array<object>} domains - Domain inventory list
   106	 * @param {object} [metrics] - Optional precomputed metrics
   107	 * @param {number} [metrics.clusterDiversity] - Fraction of domains in distinct clusters
   108	 * @returns {{ activate: boolean, reason: string, domainCount: number, clusterDiversity: number }}
   109	 */
   110	function shouldActivateResearchWave(domains, metrics) {
   111	  const domainCount = Array.isArray(domains) ? domains.length : 0;
   112	  const clusterDiversity = (metrics && typeof metrics.clusterDiversity === 'number')
   113	    ? metrics.clusterDiversity
   114	    : computeClusterDiversity(domains);
   115	
   116	  if (domainCount < RESEARCH_DOMAIN_THRESHOLD) {
   117	    return {
   118	      activate: false,
   119	      reason: `Domain count ${domainCount} below threshold ${RESEARCH_DOMAIN_THRESHOLD}`,
   120	      domainCount,
   121	      clusterDiversity,
   122	    };
   123	  }
   124	
   125	  if (clusterDiversity < CLUSTER_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD) {
   126	    return {
   127	      activate: false,
   128	      reason: `Cluster diversity ${clusterDiversity.toFixed(3)} below threshold ${CLUSTER_DIVERSITY_THRESHOLD}`,
   129	      domainCount,
   130	      clusterDiversity,
   131	    };
   132	  }
   133	
   134	  return {
   135	    activate: true,
   136	    reason: `Wave activated: ${domainCount} domains, cluster diversity ${clusterDiversity.toFixed(3)}`,
   137	    domainCount,
   138	    clusterDiversity,
   139	  };
   140	}
   141	
   142	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   143	   3. HOTSPOT INVENTORY (T-WE-NEW-2)
   144	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   145	
   146	/**
   147	 * Generate hotspot-inventory.json for review.
   148	 * Produces file ranking, directory clusters, and coverage priorities.
   149	 *
   150	 * Deterministic: same inputs always produce same output order.
   151	 *
   152	 * @param {Array<object>} files - File list with metadata
   153	 * @param {string} files[].path - File path
   154	 * @param {number} [files[].complexity] - Complexity score (higher = more complex)
   155	 * @param {number} [files[].churnRate] - Change frequency (higher = more active)
   156	 * @param {number} [files[].issueCount] - Known issues in this file
   157	 * @param {object} [metrics] - Additional scoring metrics
   158	 * @returns {object} Hotspot inventory artifact
   159	 */
   160	function generateHotspotInventory(files, metrics) {
   161	  if (!Array.isArray(files)) {
   162	    return { files: [], directories: [], segments: [], version: 'v1', generatedAt: new Date().toISOString() };
   163	  }
   164	
   165	  // Score each file for hotspot ranking
   166	  const scored = files.map(f => {
   167	    const complexity = typeof f.complexity === 'number' ? f.complexity : 1;
   168	    const churnRate = typeof f.churnRate === 'number' ? f.churnRate : 0;
   169	    const issueCount = typeof f.issueCount === 'number' ? f.issueCount : 0;
   170	
   171	    const hotspotScore = (complexity * 0.4) + (churnRate * 0.35) + (issueCount * 0.25);
   172	
   173	    return {
   174	      path: f.path || '',
   175	      directory: extractDirectory(f.path || ''),
   176	      complexity,
   177	      churnRate,
   178	      issueCount,
   179	      hotspotScore,
   180	    };
   181	  });
   182	
   183	  // Sort deterministically: by hotspotScore descending, then path ascending
   184	  scored.sort((a, b) => {
   185	    if (b.hotspotScore !== a.hotspotScore) return b.hotspotScore - a.hotspotScore;
   186	    return a.path.localeCompare(b.path);
   187	  });
   188	
   189	  // Group by directory
   190	  const dirMap = new Map();
   191	  for (const file of scored) {
   192	    if (!dirMap.has(file.directory)) {
   193	      dirMap.set(file.directory, { directory: file.directory, files: [], totalScore: 0 });
   194	    }
   195	    const entry = dirMap.get(file.directory);
   196	    entry.files.push(file);
   197	    entry.totalScore += file.hotspotScore;
   198	  }
   199	
   200	  // Sort directories by total score descending, then name ascending
   201	  const directories = Array.from(dirMap.values())
   202	    .sort((a, b) => {
   203	      if (b.totalScore !== a.totalScore) return b.totalScore - a.totalScore;
   204	      return a.directory.localeCompare(b.directory);
   205	    })
   206	    .map((d, idx) => ({
   207	      directory: d.directory,
   208	      fileCount: d.files.length,
   209	      totalScore: d.totalScore,
   210	      rank: idx + 1,
   211	    }));
   212	
   213	  return {
   214	    files: scored,
   215	    directories,
   216	    totalFiles: scored.length,
   217	    totalDirectories: directories.length,
   218	    version: 'v1',
   219	    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   220	  };
   221	}
   222	
   223	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   224	   4. DOMAIN LEDGER (T-WE-NEW-3)
   225	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   226	
   227	/**
   228	 * Generate domain-ledger.json for research.
   229	 * Produces source domains, authority levels, and cluster assignments.
   230	 *
   231	 * Deterministic: same inputs always produce same output order.
   232	 *
   233	 * @param {Array<object>} sources - Source list with metadata
   234	 * @param {string} sources[].domain - Domain identifier
   235	 * @param {string} [sources[].url] - Source URL
   236	 * @param {number} [sources[].authority] - Authority score (0.0-1.0)
   237	 * @param {string} [sources[].cluster] - Pre-assigned cluster name
   238	 * @param {string[]} [sources[].topics] - Topics this source covers
   239	 * @param {object} [metrics] - Additional clustering metrics
   240	 * @returns {object} Domain ledger artifact
   241	 */
   242	function generateDomainLedger(sources, metrics) {
   243	  if (!Array.isArray(sources)) {
   244	    return { domains: [], clusters: [], version: 'v1', generatedAt: new Date().toISOString() };
   245	  }
   246	
   247	  // Score and normalize each domain
   248	  const scored = sources.map(s => ({
   249	    domain: s.domain || '',
   250	    url: s.url || '',
   251	    authority: typeof s.authority === 'number' ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, s.authority)) : 0.5,
   252	    cluster: s.cluster || assignCluster(s),
   253	    topics: Array.isArray(s.topics) ? [...s.topics].sort() : [],
   254	  }));
   255	
   256	  // Sort deterministically: by authority descending, then domain ascending
   257	  scored.sort((a, b) => {
   258	    if (b.authority !== a.authority) return b.authority - a.authority;
   259	    return a.domain.localeCompare(b.domain);
   260	  });
   261	
   262	  // Group by cluster
   263	  const clusterMap = new Map();
   264	  for (const domain of scored) {
   265	    if (!clusterMap.has(domain.cluster)) {
   266	      clusterMap.set(domain.cluster, { cluster: domain.cluster, domains: [], totalAuthority: 0 });
   267	    }
   268	    const entry = clusterMap.get(domain.cluster);
   269	    entry.domains.push(domain);
   270	    entry.totalAuthority += domain.authority;
   271	  }
   272	
   273	  // Sort clusters by total authority descending, then name ascending
   274	  const clusters = Array.from(clusterMap.values())
   275	    .sort((a, b) => {
   276	      if (b.totalAuthority !== a.totalAuthority) return b.totalAuthority - a.totalAuthority;
   277	      return a.cluster.localeCompare(b.cluster);
   278	    })
   279	    .map((c, idx) => ({
   280	      cluster: c.cluster,
   281	      domainCount: c.domains.length,
   282	      totalAuthority: c.totalAuthority,
   283	      rank: idx + 1,
   284	    }));
   285	
   286	  return {
   287	    domains: scored,
   288	    clusters,
   289	    totalDomains: scored.length,
   290	    totalClusters: clusters.length,
   291	    version: 'v1',
   292	    generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
   293	  };
   294	}
   295	
   296	/* ---------------------------------------------------------------
   297	   5. REVIEW SEGMENTATION (T001)
   298	----------------------------------------------------------------*/
   299	
   300	/**
   301	 * Segment files for review using deterministic heuristics.
   302	 * Groups by directory, ranks by hotspot score, and produces stable

   304	 *
   305	 * @param {object} inventory - Hotspot inventory from generateHotspotInventory

   307	 * @param {number} [config.segmentSize] - Max files per segment
   308	 * @param {number} [config.maxSegments] - Maximum number of segments
   309	 * @returns {Array<object>} Ordered array of segment descriptors
   310	 */
   311	function segmentForReview(inventory, config) {
   312	  if (!inventory || !Array.isArray(inventory.files)) {
   313	    return [];
   314	  }
   315	
   316	  const cfg = config || {};
   317	  if (!cfg.segmentSize || cfg.segmentSize < 1) cfg.segmentSize = DEFAULT_SEGMENT_SIZE_REVIEW;
   318	  const segmentSize = cfg.segmentSize;
   319	  const maxSegments = cfg.maxSegments || MAX_SEGMENTS_DEFAULT;
