codex
1. Finding ID: `F-007-001`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [wave-segment-planner.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-planner.cjs:311)  
Line: `311-519`  
Title: Phase 003 still has no implemented v2 graph-enhanced segmentation path  
Description: The Phase 003 spec marks the wave executor as implemented and repeatedly promises a second segmentation mode that refines decomposition with Phase 002 graph signals. The shipped planner only implements v1 heuristics: review segmentation packs directory groups by hotspot score, and research segmentation packs cluster groups by authority. There is no graph-aware branch, no graph input contract, and no runtime selection between v1 and v2. That leaves the spec’s “implemented” v2 segmentation claims untraceable to real code.  
Evidence: Phase 003 declares “v2 graph/cluster-enhanced segmentation” in [003-wave-executor/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:53) and again in `REQ-001` / `REQ-005` / acceptance scenario 4 at lines `144`, `153`, and `180`, but `segmentForReview()` and `segmentForResearch()` only group by directories/clusters and never consume Phase 002 graph data.  
Recommendation: Either implement an explicit v2 planner path that consumes graph signals and is selected at runtime, or downgrade the Phase 003 spec/status/docs to v1-only until that path exists.

2. Finding ID: `F-007-002`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [loop_protocol.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:348)  
Line: `348-413`  
Title: Research wave protocol still documents an obsolete question-count planner  
Description: The live research wave reference teaches a different activation model than the shipped planner. Phase 003 says research waves activate from `domain-ledger.json` when scopes reach 50+ domains with cluster diversity, then proceed through plan, fan-out, prune, promote, join, and merge. The research protocol instead says wave mode is for “3+ independent key questions,” prunes below the median `newInfoRatio`, and promotes `ceil(N/2)` questions. Operators following the published protocol will not match the actual threshold-gated segment planner.  
Evidence: [003-wave-executor/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:88) defines activation gates and mandatory `domain-ledger.json`; [wave-segment-planner.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-planner.cjs:110) activates research waves at `50` domains plus `0.20` cluster diversity; the reference section at [loop_protocol.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:350) still describes a question-based median-prune model.  
Recommendation: Rewrite the research wave section to mirror the shipped planner contract, or explicitly mark the old question-based model as superseded and non-canonical.

3. Finding ID: `F-007-003`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [replay-corpus.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-corpus.cjs:215)  
Line: `215-224`  
Title: Corpus extraction drops graph and wave metrics before replay scoring  
Description: Phase 004 requires the optimizer to incorporate graph and wave metrics when traces provide them. The corpus builder only stores boolean availability flags in metadata; it does not persist any normalized `graphMetrics` or `waveMetrics` payload on the corpus entry. The replay runner later looks for top-level `corpusEntry.graphMetrics` or `corpusEntry.waveMetrics`, so the code path that is supposed to use those signals cannot fire for entries built by the shipped extractor.  
Evidence: [replay-corpus.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-corpus.cjs:218) sets only `hasGraphMetrics` / `hasWaveMetrics`, while [replay-runner.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-runner.cjs:209) checks for missing top-level `graphMetrics` / `waveMetrics`. The spec requires this behavior in [004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:140) and acceptance scenario 7 at line `169`.  
Recommendation: Persist normalized graph/wave metric payloads in `extractCorpusEntry()` and consume those exact fields in replay/rubric logic instead of only recording availability booleans.

4. Finding ID: `F-007-004`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [search.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/search.cjs:23)  
Line: `23-29`  
Title: Optimizer search space ignores the canonical tunable boundary it documents  
Description: Phase 004 says the optimizer manifest is the canonical registry of tunable fields and product boundaries. The shipped search code samples `noProgressThreshold` and `compositeStopScore` unconditionally for every run. But the manifest marks `compositeStopScore` as deep-review-only, both product config files only surface `convergenceThreshold`, `stuckThreshold`, and `maxIterations`, and the manifest leaves `configPaths` empty for `noProgressThreshold` and `compositeStopScore`. That breaks traceability from a sampled candidate back to an authoritative config file, and it lets research-mode optimization tune a review-only field.  
Evidence: [search.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/search.cjs:23) samples five tunables; [optimizer-manifest.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/optimizer-manifest.json:42) lists `noProgressThreshold` and `compositeStopScore`, but `configPaths` are empty and `compositeStopScore` is limited to `deep-review`; [deep_research_config.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json:60) and [deep_review_config.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json:77) do not expose those fields in their optimizer-managed lists.  
Recommendation: Build per-product parameter spaces from the manifest, require each tunable to resolve to a concrete config path, and remove or defer fields that are not yet surfaced in canonical product configs.

**Iteration Summary**  
Total findings: `4`  
Breakdown: `0 P0`, `4 P1`, `0 P2`

**Coverage Assessment**  
Reviewed about `95%` of the requested focus area: both phase specs, all five `wave-*.cjs` modules, all five `optimizer/*.cjs` modules plus `optimizer-manifest.json`, the deep research/review config surfaces, relevant loop/convergence references, command docs, and the phase-specific test suites.

**Confidence**  
`high`

**Next Priorities**  
Inspect the Phase 003/004 YAML workflow assets and strategy/state-format mirrors next, especially `spec_kit_deep-research_*.yaml`, `spec_kit_deep-review_*.yaml`, and the state-format docs, to see whether the same wave/optimizer drift is duplicated in the orchestration layer and packet-facing artifacts.

The shipped planner only implements v1 heuristics: review segmentation packs directory groups by hotspot score, and research segmentation packs cluster groups by authority. There is no graph-aware branch, no graph input contract, and no runtime selection between v1 and v2. That leaves the spec’s “implemented” v2 segmentation claims untraceable to real code.  
Evidence: Phase 003 declares “v2 graph/cluster-enhanced segmentation” in [003-wave-executor/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:53) and again in `REQ-001` / `REQ-005` / acceptance scenario 4 at lines `144`, `153`, and `180`, but `segmentForReview()` and `segmentForResearch()` only group by directories/clusters and never consume Phase 002 graph data.  
Recommendation: Either implement an explicit v2 planner path that consumes graph signals and is selected at runtime, or downgrade the Phase 003 spec/status/docs to v1-only until that path exists.

2. Finding ID: `F-007-002`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [loop_protocol.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:348)  
Line: `348-413`  
Title: Research wave protocol still documents an obsolete question-count planner  
Description: The live research wave reference teaches a different activation model than the shipped planner. Phase 003 says research waves activate from `domain-ledger.json` when scopes reach 50+ domains with cluster diversity, then proceed through plan, fan-out, prune, promote, join, and merge. The research protocol instead says wave mode is for “3+ independent key questions,” prunes below the median `newInfoRatio`, and promotes `ceil(N/2)` questions. Operators following the published protocol will not match the actual threshold-gated segment planner.  
Evidence: [003-wave-executor/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/003-wave-executor/spec.md:88) defines activation gates and mandatory `domain-ledger.json`; [wave-segment-planner.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/lib/wave-segment-planner.cjs:110) activates research waves at `50` domains plus `0.20` cluster diversity; the reference section at [loop_protocol.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:350) still describes a question-based median-prune model.  
Recommendation: Rewrite the research wave section to mirror the shipped planner contract, or explicitly mark the old question-based model as superseded and non-canonical.

3. Finding ID: `F-007-003`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [replay-corpus.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-corpus.cjs:215)  
Line: `215-224`  
Title: Corpus extraction drops graph and wave metrics before replay scoring  
Description: Phase 004 requires the optimizer to incorporate graph and wave metrics when traces provide them. The corpus builder only stores boolean availability flags in metadata; it does not persist any normalized `graphMetrics` or `waveMetrics` payload on the corpus entry. The replay runner later looks for top-level `corpusEntry.graphMetrics` or `corpusEntry.waveMetrics`, so the code path that is supposed to use those signals cannot fire for entries built by the shipped extractor.  
Evidence: [replay-corpus.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-corpus.cjs:218) sets only `hasGraphMetrics` / `hasWaveMetrics`, while [replay-runner.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/replay-runner.cjs:209) checks for missing top-level `graphMetrics` / `waveMetrics`. The spec requires this behavior in [004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/spec.md:140) and acceptance scenario 7 at line `169`.  
Recommendation: Persist normalized graph/wave metric payloads in `extractCorpusEntry()` and consume those exact fields in replay/rubric logic instead of only recording availability booleans.

4. Finding ID: `F-007-004`  
Severity: `P1`  
Dimension: `traceability`  
File: [search.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/search.cjs:23)  
Line: `23-29`  
Title: Optimizer search space ignores the canonical tunable boundary it documents  
Description: Phase 004 says the optimizer manifest is the canonical registry of tunable fields and product boundaries. The shipped search code samples `noProgressThreshold` and `compositeStopScore` unconditionally for every run. But the manifest marks `compositeStopScore` as deep-review-only, both product config files only surface `convergenceThreshold`, `stuckThreshold`, and `maxIterations`, and the manifest leaves `configPaths` empty for `noProgressThreshold` and `compositeStopScore`. That breaks traceability from a sampled candidate back to an authoritative config file, and it lets research-mode optimization tune a review-only field.  
Evidence: [search.cjs](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/search.cjs:23) samples five tunables; [optimizer-manifest.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/optimizer/optimizer-manifest.json:42) lists `noProgressThreshold` and `compositeStopScore`, but `configPaths` are empty and `compositeStopScore` is limited to `deep-review`; [deep_research_config.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/deep_research_config.json:60) and [deep_review_config.json](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json:77) do not expose those fields in their optimizer-managed lists.  
Recommendation: Build per-product parameter spaces from the manifest, require each tunable to resolve to a concrete config path, and remove or defer fields that are not yet surfaced in canonical product configs.
